tldr: Move from Cloudinary's combined storage+transform platform to Imgix, which transforms and delivers images that you keep in your own origin storage (e.g. S3).
why_switch: Lower lock-in: your originals live in your own bucket, Imgix only transforms/delivers
URL-based transformation API with strong real-time format negotiation (WebP/AVIF)
Cleaner separation of storage and delivery concerns
Often simpler billing for transform/CDN-only workloads
Easier to swap delivery providers later because originals are portable
what_to_export: All original assets from the Cloudinary Media Library
List of asset URLs referenced in production
Upload preset configurations (to recreate ingest pipeline)
Any derived assets you depend on
export_method: Use Cloudinary's Admin API or CLI to bulk-download originals into your own object storage (typically S3 or compatible). Maintain a manifest of public_id to new origin path. Consult Cloudinary's official docs for current bulk export procedures.
import_method: Upload originals to a supported origin (commonly S3 or compatible object storage), then create an Imgix Source pointing at that origin. Rewrite transformed URLs from Cloudinary syntax to Imgix's URL parameter syntax per their official docs. Update application/CMS references to the new Imgix delivery domain.

concept_mapping.note: Imgix reads from your origin, doesn't store originals
Different syntax — rewrite all transformed URLs
Imgix supports automatic format/quality via params
Configure signing key per Imgix docs
Replace with your own ingest pipeline
If you rely on Cloudinary video, check Imgix coverage

gotchas: You now own the origin storage — provision and secure it before migration
Every transformed URL in your codebase needs a syntax rewrite
Video features differ from Cloudinary — validate before cutover
Cache invalidation strategy changes when Imgix sits in front of your origin
Plan a parallel-running window to catch any missed URL references
Confirm pricing model for transformations and origin fetches at your scale

email.subject: Cancelling Cloudinary subscription — moving to Imgix
email.body:
Hi Cloudinary team,

We're moving our image delivery to Imgix backed by our own origin storage. Please let me know how to cancel our subscription at the end of the current billing cycle, and the recommended approach for a full bulk export of our originals via the Admin API.

Thanks for the service.

Best regards
